narrative: Fed's Williams and Barr signal policy stability and balance sheet caution as Kevin Warsh's appointment cools rate cut hopes—USD leads G8 (+0.71% avg) while GBP lags (-0.74% avg) on Labour turmoil. USD/JPY firms to 158.47 on a 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage, though 25d RR at -1.69 shows strong put skew for downside protection; gold lower -1.35% near $4,622; equities +0.77%; DXY +0.21%; VIX 17.3. Watch RBNZ Rate Decision May 27 at 02:00 UTC.
